title: Peacock Bar
html:
Peacock Bar

A bar — most likely in Grants Pass or somewhere in the southern Oregon orbit, given the timing — that anchors one of the most consequential single-incident notes in the corpus: 2025 03 01 peacock bar incident. The Peacock entry in the wiki is deliberately conservative on detail per the personal-stub rule; what's worth keeping is what the event signifies in the broader graph, not the specifics.

The incident itself: late February 2025, a chance encounter at the Peacock with a former partner after roughly 10 months of no contact, an escalation that involved punches, a taser, a bouncer, restraint, handcuffs, transparent answers to police, vitals checked, and Yahir released. The morning-after note is the cleanest single demonstration of his two writing modes: a first-pass note as letter to self in second-person present tense (raw, sprawling, processing in real time) followed by a sanitized third-person rewrite (modified through 2025-03-05) that reads as lawyer-ready timeline documentation, careful with verbs, framing his side as defensive. The before/after pair is itself a stylistic specimen worth tagging — Apple Notes used not for brainstorming but for legal-grade documentation.

The Peacock matters in the graph because it is load-bearing for the 2025 mood. Read against 2025 09 23 how im scraping by six months later, the financial and physical isolation continue. Read against the 2025 04 20 people i am indebted to for helping me gratitude list (written ~7 weeks after the incident — was that timing significant?), the relationship-map gets formalized in the wake of conflict. Read against the Austin move executed in September 2025 (2025 05 30 todos), the Peacock arguably accelerates the desire to leave the state. The post-breakup-conflict thread the incident sits in (post breakup conflict) is one of the heavier threads in the corpus and is closer to the founder loneliness 2022 2026 arc than to the everyday-Yahir register.

Geographically the Peacock is one of two bars confirmed in the surfaced corpus (the Corvallis date-vault from 2021 10 25 places for a date mentions Crowbar, but Peacock is not on that list — meaning it's likely a Grants Pass or non-Corvallis venue, possibly somewhere Yahir went specifically because his ex was there, possibly chance). Confirming the city would tighten the pre-Austin chronology, but the conservatism rule holds: don't speculate further than the source supports.
